Strong teachers
don't automatically
become great mentors.

MSA is the first mentor certification program built on a complete methodology — pairing temperament-based coaching intelligence with a rigorous classroom observation framework — so your mentors don't just encourage teachers. They transform them.

The Problem

Your best teachers
deserve more than
good intentions.

Most early childhood programs invest in mentorship — but without a shared methodology, mentors improvise. They rely on intuition, personality, and the way they were mentored themselves. Some of it works. Much of it doesn't.

What's missing isn't commitment. It's a system — one that tells mentors how to read the teacher in front of them, what to look for in the classroom, and exactly how to turn an observation into a coaching conversation that produces real change.

  • →
    Mentors need coaching tools as much as teachers need academic ones
  • →
    One-size-fits-all feedback reaches some teachers and alienates others
  • →
    Observation without a framework produces notes, not growth
  • →
    Strong programs retain teachers — and mentorship is how retention happens
"This is exactly what we needed. Someone who could teach our leaders how to mentor others — to keep our teams growing together."
Kirsten Swem · Center Director
What changes when mentors are trained
  • ✓
    Teachers feel seen — which is the first reason they stay
  • ✓
    Feedback lands because it's tailored to how that teacher learns
  • ✓
    Classroom quality improves because observations are purposeful
  • ✓
    Programs build a mentoring culture that sustains itself

The MSA Methodology

Two tools.
One complete system.

MSA is built on the integration of two proprietary frameworks — each powerful alone, and transformational together.

01
SELCS Framework

Know who
you're coaching.

The SELCS instrument identifies four distinct mentee temperament styles — Director, Inspirer, Sustainer, and Cultivator — each of which responds differently to feedback, challenge, and encouragement.

  • Temperament assessment for mentees
  • Scenario-based coaching training for mentors
  • Style-matched feedback strategies for all four types
  • Awareness of your own mentoring temperament
02
Quality Interaction Framework

Know what
you're observing.

The QIF is MSA's classroom observation instrument — a structured tool for scoring teacher-child interactions across three domains, tied to indicators aligned with CLASS-based research in early childhood.

  • Three observation domains: Emotional Climate, Environment, Instructional Depth
  • 1–7 scoring across 12 indicators with observable descriptors
  • Structured coaching debrief tied directly to scores
  • Research-anchored goal-setting for each indicator
03
The Integration

Know how
to connect them.

MSA training teaches mentors to use QIF observation data and SELCS temperament profiles together — so the debrief after every observation is informed by what was seen in the classroom AND how that specific teacher is most likely to receive it.

  • Observation-to-debrief coaching framework
  • SELCS-filtered feedback language by domain
  • Goal-setting protocol matched to mentee style
  • Weekly observation targets between formal visits
04
The Outcome

Mentors who
move teachers forward.

When mentors know how to read both the classroom and the person in it, their conversations stop being check-ins and start being turning points. Teachers grow faster, feel more supported, and stay longer.

  • Certified mentors equipped with a complete methodology
  • Stronger retention through purposeful mentoring culture
  • Measurable classroom quality improvement over time
  • A shared language that strengthens your entire team
The Certification Program

Everything your mentors
need to lead with confidence.

The MSA Mentor Certification is a 24-hour professional development program — delivered through live sessions, on-demand learning, and hands-on field application.

🎓

SELCS Scenario Training

Interactive, scenario-based modules that teach mentors to identify temperament styles and adjust their coaching approach in real time.

🔍

QIF Observation Instrument

A structured, research-grounded classroom observation tool with scoring, debrief protocols, and coaching conversation guides.

📋

Mentor Meeting Framework

A structured agenda for mentor-mentee meetings — with objectives built from observation data and tailored to each teacher's growth edge.

🎥

Live & Recorded Sessions

A blend of live cohort learning and on-demand resources — accessible on your schedule and referenceable throughout your mentoring practice.

What's
included.

The full MSA Mentor Certification gives your team everything they need — tools, training, and a framework they'll use in every mentoring relationship.

24 hours of
instruction
  • ✓
    SELCS temperament assessment for mentees
  • ✓
    SELCS scenario-based training modules (all four styles)
  • ✓
    QIF classroom observation instrument with scoring guide
  • ✓
    Observation-to-debrief coaching protocol
  • ✓
    Mentor-mentee meeting framework with session objectives
  • ✓
    Live cohort sessions with Rebecca & Mary
  • ✓
    On-demand video library & resource downloads
  • ✓
    MSA Mentor Certification credential upon completion
How It Works

From application
to certified mentors
in your program.

We work directly with program directors to bring MSA certification to your team — cohort by cohort, with ongoing support throughout.

1

Apply

Submit your beta application. We'll schedule a brief conversation to understand your program, your team, and your mentoring goals. We're selective — this is a relationship, not a transaction.

2

Cohort

Your selected mentors join an MSA cohort — a small, intentional group that moves through the certification together with live sessions, peer learning, and field application in your classrooms.

3

Certified

Upon completing 24 hours of instruction and demonstrating field competency, your mentors earn the MSA Mentor Certification — and your program has a mentoring methodology that belongs to it permanently.

Currently accepting beta applications. Beta participants receive full access to the MSA certification program at a significantly reduced investment in exchange for structured feedback that helps us refine the final product. Cohort sizes are intentionally small. If you're interested, apply early.
The Founders

Two decades of experience.
One shared mission.

Co-Founder

Rebecca Munlyn

Leadership Development Consultant · Founder & CEO, Inspired Growth LLC

Rebecca Munlyn is the co-founder of the Mentor Success Academy as well as the founder and CEO of Inspired Growth, LLC, a consulting firm which partners with organizations to unlock potential and expand capacity of leaders and teams to support healthy organizational growth. She integrates strategies with neuro-linguistic, data-driven, and research-based tools to facilitate self-awareness, communication, and increased performance.

Rebecca is a leader and team development consultant with a proven record as a committed and passionate advocate for creating safe spaces for all voices to be heard and valued. She is gifted in empowering individuals to identify, access, and leverage individual strengths to create lives free of limits — with more than 20 years of diverse coaching and consulting experience within corporate and nonprofit organizations.

Her innovative approach to implementing efficient systems, sustainable processes, and navigating adult learning strategies are informed by her Bachelor of Industrial Engineering (Georgia Tech) and Master of Education (Columbus State University). Rebecca's passion for connection and relationship is the foundation upon which the process of self-discovery and empowerment is explored, embraced, and facilitates transformational growth — both professionally and personally.

🎓B.S. Industrial Engineering · Georgia Tech
🎓M.Ed. · Columbus State University
✦20+ Years Coaching & Consulting
Co-Founder

Mary Wardlaw

Early Childhood Education Specialist · Founder, The Children's Center & National CDA Training

Mary Wardlaw is the co-founder of Mentor Success Academy and a lifelong advocate for excellence in early childhood education. Mary began her journey in the field as an assistant preschool teacher in 1992, advancing to lead teacher, program director, and eventually founding The Children's Center in 2002 with just three teachers and 17 students. Today, she leads three thriving locations in southwest Michigan, serving over 450 students with a team of 80 dedicated staff.

In 2019, Mary expanded her impact by founding National CDA Training, helping hundreds of early childhood educators earn their Child Development Associate (CDA) credentials. Since 2023, her research has centered on the training, mentoring, and retention of high-quality early childhood teachers — work that directly inspired the creation of Mentor Success Academy to develop the next generation of confident, capable, and resilient educators.

Mary holds a bachelor's degree in Child Development (2004), a master's degree in Business Administration (2006), and completed her Education Specialist Degree in 2025. She is currently pursuing her Ph.D. in Early Childhood Education.

🎓B.S. Child Development · 2004
🎓MBA · 2006
🎓Ed.S. · 2025 · Ph.D. Candidate, Early Childhood Education
✦Founder, The Children's Center (3 locations, 450+ students)
✦Founder, National CDA Training
